verses.text: At Melita the barbarous people show kindness, kindling a fire in the rain. Unexpected kindness. Heathen strangers welcome the shipwrecked.
At Melita the barbarous people show kindness, kindling a fire in the rain. Unexpected kindness. Heathen strangers welcome the shipwrecked.
A viper fastens on Paul's hand; he shakes it off unhurt; they say he is a god. Preserved. God guards him from the serpent's bite.
A viper fastens on Paul's hand; he shakes it off unhurt; they say he is a god. Preserved. God guards him from the serpent's bite.
A viper fastens on Paul's hand; he shakes it off unhurt; they say he is a god. Preserved. God guards him from the serpent's bite.
A viper fastens on Paul's hand; he shakes it off unhurt; they say he is a god. Preserved. God guards him from the serpent's bite.
Paul heals the father of Publius and many others; they honour them with gifts. Healing mercy. The island is blessed by the apostle's stay.
Paul heals the father of Publius and many others; they honour them with gifts. Healing mercy. The island is blessed by the apostle's stay.
Paul heals the father of Publius and many others; they honour them with gifts. Healing mercy. The island is blessed by the apostle's stay.
Paul heals the father of Publius and many others; they honour them with gifts. Healing mercy. The island is blessed by the apostle's stay.
After three months they sail; at Puteoli they find brethren and tarry seven days. Fellowship found. Believers are everywhere along the way.
After three months they sail; at Puteoli they find brethren and tarry seven days. Fellowship found. Believers are everywhere along the way.
After three months they sail; at Puteoli they find brethren and tarry seven days. Fellowship found. Believers are everywhere along the way.
After three months they sail; at Puteoli they find brethren and tarry seven days. Fellowship found. Believers are everywhere along the way.
Brethren come from Rome to meet them; 'he thanked God, and took courage.' Encouraged. The sight of fellow saints lifts his heart.
Brethren come from Rome to meet them; 'he thanked God, and took courage.' Encouraged. The sight of fellow saints lifts his heart.
Paul tells the Jews he has done nothing against the people, yet is bound 'for the hope of Israel.' His defence. He suffers for the very hope they share.
Paul tells the Jews he has done nothing against the people, yet is bound 'for the hope of Israel.' His defence. He suffers for the very hope they share.
Paul tells the Jews he has done nothing against the people, yet is bound 'for the hope of Israel.' His defence. He suffers for the very hope they share.
Paul tells the Jews he has done nothing against the people, yet is bound 'for the hope of Israel.' His defence. He suffers for the very hope they share.
They have heard no evil of him, but desire to hear, for 'this sect is every where spoken against.' Curious. They will judge the matter for themselves.
They have heard no evil of him, but desire to hear, for 'this sect is every where spoken against.' Curious. They will judge the matter for themselves.
He expounds the kingdom from morning till evening; some believe, some believe not. Divided hearing. The same word draws faith and unbelief.
He expounds the kingdom from morning till evening; some believe, some believe not. Divided hearing. The same word draws faith and unbelief.
Well spake Isaiah, hearing ye shall not understand; 'their heart is waxed gross.' Judicial blindness. The hardened cannot perceive the truth.
Well spake Isaiah, hearing ye shall not understand; 'their heart is waxed gross.' Judicial blindness. The hardened cannot perceive the truth.
Well spake Isaiah, hearing ye shall not understand; 'their heart is waxed gross.' Judicial blindness. The hardened cannot perceive the truth.
'The salvation of God is sent unto the Gentiles, and... they will hear it.' Open door. Where Israel refuses, the nations receive.
'The salvation of God is sent unto the Gentiles, and... they will hear it.' Open door. Where Israel refuses, the nations receive.
Paul dwells two years in his own house, 'preaching the kingdom of God... no man forbidding him.' Unhindered gospel. The book closes with the word advancing freely.
